channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Exploring Multi-Sensory Architecture for Inclusive Public Spaces
item.description: Erin Naidoo, a recent master’s graduate from the University of KwaZulu-Natal, conducted research challenging the traditional vision-focused approach in architecture. Her study focused on how visually ...

item.title: Man in his 30s arrested after car rams into pedestrians in Modena, injuring eight
item.description: Eight people were injured, two of them seriously, after a man in his 30s drove a car into a group of pedestrians in the northern Italian city of Modena on Saturday. The driver, an Italian national of ...

item.title: Execution of William Kemmler: The First Use of the Electric Chair in 1890
item.description: William Kemmler (1860–1890) was an American produce merchant who became the first person in history executed by electric chair. In March 1889, Kemmler murdered his common-law wife, Matilda Ziegler, du...

item.title: Bulgaria Claims Victory at the 70th Eurovision Song Contest with 'Bangaranga'
item.description: Bulgaria emerged as the winner of the 70th edition of the Eurovision Song Contest, held in Vienna, captivating audiences with their high-energy party anthem, 'Bangaranga.' The competition, which has g...

item.title: Indian Leaders in Kashmir Support Dialogue with Pakistan Amid Rising Peace Calls
item.description: ISLAMABAD – Following the first anniversary of Marka-i-Haq, there has been a noticeable shift in dialogue sentiment between India and Pakistan. Calls for engagement have grown louder, especially from ...

item.title: Large London protests highlight tensions over immigration and Palestinian solidarity
item.description: On Saturday, central London saw tens of thousands of participants in two major demonstrations. One protest, organised by anti-Islam activist Stephen Yaxley-Lennon, also known as Tommy Robinson, oppose...

item.title: Motorola India files lawsuit against social media platforms over negative reviews
item.description: Motorola India has initiated legal proceedings against major tech companies and their platforms, including Google, Meta, X, YouTube, Instagram, Facebook, and Threads, over more than 360 user-generated...

item.title: Santa Monica Woman Dies After Crawling Under a Stopped Metro Bus
item.description: A tragic incident occurred in Santa Monica on Friday afternoon when a woman was killed after crawling underneath a stopped Metro bus. According to the Santa Monica Police Department, the fatal collisi...

item.title: Understanding 'Santacruzan' as the Word of the Day on May 17, 2026
item.description: The ABS-CBN Lifestyle feature for May 17, 2026, introduces 'santacruzan' as the Word of the Day. The term refers to a traditional Filipino cultural and religious procession held in May, commemorating ...

item.title: Senate Rules Prevent Trump’s White House Ballroom Funding in Immigration Bill
item.description: President Donald Trump's proposal for a new White House ballroom has encountered a significant procedural obstacle in the Senate. The Senate parliamentarian ruled that $1 billion requested for the bal...

item.title: Regina Kennel and Obedience Club marks 110 years with annual dog trials
item.description: The Regina Kennel and Obedience Club recently celebrated its 110th anniversary, marking over a century of dedication to dog training and canine competitions in the region. As part of the celebrations,...

item.title: Alberta separatist leader appeals court ruling blocking independence petition
item.description: Mitch Sylvestre, leader of the Stay Free Alberta petition and the Alberta Prosperity Project, is appealing a decision by a Court of King’s Bench judge that overturned approval of his group’s petition ...

item.title: Lions clinch URC quarter-finals but struggle in defeat to Munster
item.description: The Lions have officially secured a spot in the United Rugby Championship (URC) quarter-finals despite losing 24-17 to Munster at Thomond Park on Saturday night. With qualification confirmed prior to ...

item.title: Rousey and Carano Set to Return from Retirement for MMA Super-Fight in California
item.description: Ronda Rousey and Gina Carano, both former MMA stars, are set to come out of retirement for an unexpected fight in California. Rousey, a former UFC bantamweight champion, has not competed since her 201...

item.title: Bill Cassidy eliminated from Louisiana Senate primary as Letlow and Fleming advance to runoff
item.description: In the 2026 Louisiana Republican Senate primary, incumbent Senator Bill Cassidy failed to secure enough support to reach the runoff, marking a significant political setback. Cassidy, who faced critici...

item.title: Napoleon Solo Wins the Preakness Stakes at Laurel Park, Defeating Iron Honor
item.description: Napoleon Solo claimed victory in the Preakness Stakes at Laurel Park, holding off Iron Honor by 1 1/4 lengths. This marked his first win of the year after two fifth-place finishes in earlier races. Wi...

item.title: U.S. prosecutors link Iraqi national to Toronto U.S. consulate shooting and other attacks as part of Iranian-backed network
item.description: U.S. prosecutors have connected an Iraqi national, Mohammad Baqer Saad Dawood Al-Saadi, to a shooting outside the U.S. consulate in downtown Toronto on March 10, 2026. Al-Saadi, who holds senior roles...

item.title: Quebec Premier Christine Fréchette Visits Paris to Strengthen Economic Ties with France
item.description: Quebec Premier Christine Fréchette has embarked on an official visit to Paris, where she is scheduled to meet French President Emmanuel Macron at the Élysée Palace. The mission, running until May 20, ...

item.title: Mike Sonko Shares Health Check-Up Results; Ngina Kenyatta's Green Experience Restaurant Detailed
item.description: This week, TUKO.co.ke highlighted some of the major celebrity stories in Kenya. Former Nairobi Governor Mike Sonko went public with his HIV status after undergoing routine medical tests as part of his...

item.title: Elderly Andhra couple forcibly removed during protest over airport land compensation
item.description: In Andhra Pradesh’s Vizianagaram district, an elderly couple, Baireddy Appala Narasaiah and Gouthamma, both in their sixties, were forcibly lifted in an excavator bucket during a protest against the g...
